question_text: Explain why ultrasound with a frequency of 40 kHz cannot be heard by humans, even though it is still a sound wave.
correct_answer: Humans can only hear sounds with frequencies between about 20 Hz and 20,000 Hz. Ultrasound at 40 kHz (40,000 Hz) has a frequency too high for human ears to detect, even though it is a real sound wave that can travel through air and other materials.
explanation: The human ear has a limited range of frequencies it can detect. Ultrasound frequencies are above this range, so we cannot hear them, but animals like dogs and bats can. The sound wave itself is real; our ears just cannot respond to such high frequencies.
